daily_brief: The Middle East conflict remains the dominant global story, with Israel continuing strikes in southern Lebanon despite a newly announced US-brokered ceasefire that Hezbollah has emphatically rejected [BBC], [France 24], [The Hindu]. The strikes killed seven overnight in Tyre, including near a hospital, as the UN doubled its Lebanon aid appeal to $640 million [France 24]. Meanwhile, Ukraine's President Zelensky issued an open letter proposing direct talks with Putin, acknowledging that US attention is 'fully focused on Iran' [BBC]. In East Africa, the Ebola outbreak in DRC continues to strain response efforts, with WHO confirming the outbreak may have begun as early as January and rebel attacks near Beni killing over 30 people [The Guardian]. The crisis has prompted controversy over US plans for an American-only quarantine facility in Kenya. On technology, AI company Anthropic called for a global pause on frontier AI development, warning that cutting-edge systems are showing signs they could escape human control [France 24]. In the Balkans, EU leaders gathered in Montenegro to advance Western Balkans enlargement amid protests in Albania over a Kushner-linked resort development [RFI], [BBC], [Al Jazeera].

top_stories.headline: Israel Continues Lebanon Strikes Despite Ceasefire; Hezbollah Rejects US-Brokered Deal
bullets: Israeli strikes overnight in Tyre killed seven people, including four near Jabal Amel hospital, despite a ceasefire agreement announced by Israeli and Lebanese envoys in Washington
Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected the conditional truce as 'absurd, humiliating and insulting,' demanding a full Israeli withdrawal and comprehensive ceasefire
The UN doubled its Lebanon aid appeal to $640 million over six months, citing a 'severe and deteriorating' humanitarian crisis with over one million displaced
President Trump said he spoke with both Hezbollah and Netanyahu, claiming 'progress was being made'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized humanitarian impact and WFP hunger warnings

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on Hezbollah rejection and Trump's diplomatic claims

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed casualty figures and hospital damage

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Highlighted internal Lebanese divisions and federalism debates

top_stories.headline: Zelensky Proposes Direct Putin Meeting, Acknowledges US Focus on Iran
bullets: Ukrainian President Zelensky sent an open letter to Putin proposing face-to-face talks and a full ceasefire during negotiations
The letter acknowledged that the US 'is fully focused on the issue of Iran' and said it would be 'wrong to simply wait' for European attention to return
Putin rejected a ceasefire during negotiations; Kremlin said Zelensky was welcome to meet in Moscow
Trump endorsed the idea of a meeting, saying 'it would be great' if the two leaders met

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Noted the defiant, mocking tone of Zelensky's letter

top_stories.headline: DRC Ebola Outbreak May Have Started in January; Rebel Attacks Kill 30 Near Outbreak Zone
bullets: WHO chief Tedros said the Ebola outbreak could have begun as early as January, giving the virus 'a big head start' with 344 confirmed cases and 60 deaths in DRC
ADF militia attacks around Beni killed over 30 people in recent days, with beheadings reported and hundreds displaced
Contact tracing is at only 45% due to insecurity; WHO says it needs to reach 90% to control the outbreak
US plans for an American-only Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya drew criticism from former CDC officials and the CDC workers' union

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Provided extensive coverage across multiple articles on outbreak origins, rebel attacks, and US quarantine controversy

top_stories.headline: Anthropic Calls for Global Pause on Frontier AI Development
bullets: AI company Anthropic warned that cutting-edge AI models are showing signs they could escape human control
The company said a global slowdown would 'likely be a good thing' but acknowledged unilateral action would simply let rivals race ahead
Anthropic called for coordination among major AI companies in the US and China with verifiable rules
The company has faced pushback from industry rivals and White House officials

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the 'narrowing human role' and control concerns

top_stories.headline: EU-Western Balkans Summit Advances Enlargement Amid Albania Kushner Resort Protests
bullets: EU and Western Balkan leaders gathered in Tivat, Montenegro for a summit focused on enlargement for six candidate countries
European Council President Costa said 'the momentum is there' for enlargement, with discussions on economic reforms and security
Protests erupted in Albania over a luxury resort development on Sazan island linked to Jared Kushner's investment firm
Protesters cited environmental concerns for protected wetlands and flamingo habitats; demonstrations targeted both the government and the project

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focused on summit diplomacy and enlargement timeline

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Emphasized protest angle and environmental concerns

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Ivanka Trump's comments and local opposition narrative

additional_stories.headline: Forty-nine people died of thirst in Niger's Sahara desert after their truck broke down en route from Mali
French-Iranian author and filmmaker Marjane Satrapi, creator of Persepolis, died at age 56
Chinese President Xi Jinping will visit North Korea June 8-9, his first trip in seven years
A maritime drone 'of the type used in Ukraine' self-detonated in Romania's Constanta port without casualties
UK military chief warns Britain faces most dangerous period since Cold War with increased Russian incursions
Hungary dropped charges against Pride march organizers following EU court ruling against anti-LGBTQ law
South Korean stocks fell over 5% as Samsung and SK Hynix shares dropped following Broadcom's $285 billion market value loss
Reserve Bank of India held rates at 5.25% but raised inflation forecast amid energy crisis concerns
Putin pledged non-interference in India-China border disputes, expressing confidence Modi and Xi will resolve issues
Dutch police arrested four men investigating suspected drugging and sexual assaults of multiple women filmed on camera
